page: Amazon Web Services g6e.2xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.4 GHz, delivering 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU with 45 GB of VRAM, and 450 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 5 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in large language model inference speeds for prompt processing and text generation using small and medium models. Prompt processing for large models is also strong, placing in the top 25%. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is strong, while multi-core CPU performance, memory read bandwidth, and general compute benchmarks fall into the average tier. However, a notable performance tradeoff is the large block memory write bandwidth, which is classified in the bottom 25%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the g6e.2xlarge offers a dense resource profile by bundling dedicated AMD EPYC compute, high-capacity GPU VRAM, and local NVMe storage on a single instance. This makes it highly suitable for workloads such as machine learning inference, graphics-intensive rendering, and database operations that require a balance of GPU acceleration and local high-speed storage.
description: Amazon Web Services g6e.2xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance featuring an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs, 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a baseline network speed of 5 Gbps. It includes a dedicated NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU with 45 GB of VRAM and 450 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier capabilities in LLM inference for small and medium models, alongside strong single-core CPU performance. Conversely, large-block memory write bandwidth represents a performance tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 25%. Qualitatively, the instance provides a resource-dense configuration for GPU-accelerated tasks. It is highly suited for workloads such as machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and database operations requiring local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.2xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring an AMD EPYC CPU, 64 GB RAM, 450 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU with 45 GB VRAM, optimized for LLM inference and graphics workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.2xlarge is an x86_64 server with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, 450 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU for accelerated workloads.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, high-capacity VRAM, and local NVMe storage for demanding machine learning inference.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-vCPU AMD EPYC 7R13 processor and AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Equipped with one NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU featuring 45 GB of VRAM.
Includes 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 450 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Delivers top-tier benchmark performance for small and medium LLM inference.
Features 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and strong single-core CPU performance.
Best suited for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and database workloads.
